ops.title: Investigate: deletion-only .env changes miss the watch fast path (ride the 15-min tick)
ops.description:
Live-verified on the two-host mesh (reposync 0.15.0, fsnotify manifests on BOTH hosts, 2026-07-14): a rewrite of an existing tracked-repo `.env` that only REMOVES keys converges via the 15-min tick (688s laptop→peer step d; 556s peer→laptop cleanup truncate), while creates (77s/171s), value updates (108s), and one laptop truncate-to-empty (46s) took the debounced watch path. Correctness is unaffected — the tick floor converges everything, tombstones and LWW verified byte-identical in the sidecars — this is latency only.

Facts to reconcile: the laptop serve log shows `watchbackend: rewatching dir ... /Users/yasyf/Code/repo-sync/.env` lines within 4-9s of writes; step (f)'s truncate on the laptop DID fast-path 46s while the peer's cleanup truncate did not; the values-only `env.Digest` excludes tombstones by design, but a key removal still changes the digest (fewer present keys), so the fingerprint should move. Suspects: (1) synckit fsnotify per-file watch lifecycle — whether the 30s sweep's fires-on-appear also arms a Write watch, and whether a temp+rename apply leaves the peer watch dead until sweep (reposync's known inode-swap gap) in a way that correlates with these specific legs; (2) the engine's apply-stable fingerprint record/dedupe ordering around a converge that itself rewrites the file; (3) kqueue truncate/O_TRUNC event semantics on darwin for file-level fsnotify watches. Needs: read synckit/watchbackend/fsnotify.go + watch/engine.go against both hosts' serve logs from 02:10-02:55 on 2026-07-14 (laptop `~/Library/Logs/synckit/com.github.yasyf.synckit.serve.log`, same on peer), then decide whether the fix is a synckit sweep/arm change or a reposync WatchDirs shape change (file paths are the only granular option — the fsnotify backend watches directories recursively).

ops.text: Root cause identified with serve-log + code citation for why deletion-only rewrites missed the debounced path on both hosts
Fix landed (synckit or reposync) or explicit accept-with-docs decision recorded; deletion propagation measured under 3 min on the live mesh afterwards
